goal: Add an explicit activation-lock boundary for provider calls, runtime dry-run, and write actions based on Maya-gate and provider/runtime preflight evidence, without executing providers, runtime, writes, permit issuance, merges, or deploys.
scope_out: Do not call providers, execute runtime, mount runtime execution, issue permits, write DB/files/GitHub, create PRs from the app, merge, deploy, change package/workflow files, or reopen retired sandbox write routes.
claims: Bluepilot can evaluate provider-call activation-lock readiness from provider/runtime and Maya-gate evidence.
Bluepilot can evaluate runtime dry-run activation-lock readiness while keeping runtime execution and route mount closed.
Bluepilot can evaluate write-action activation-lock readiness from Maya corridor, permit, target, path, and content-hash evidence while keeping writes and permit issuance closed.
Even when activationLockReady is true, providerExecutionAllowed, runtimeExecutionAllowed, writeExecutionAllowed, runtimeRouteMountAllowed, and permitIssueAllowed remain false.
assumptions: Actual provider calls, runtime execution, write execution, and permit issuance require later explicit executor locks.
Live Maya reachability remains owned by /health/maya-gate and dry evidence remains owned by the existing Maya/provider-runtime preflights.
dod: GET /probe/activation-lock-contract returns the closed activation boundary.
POST /probe/activation-lock-preflight validates provider, runtime, and write activation-lock evidence without execution.
Meta exposes the new activation-lock surfaces.
Focused tests, typecheck, task-lock verification, diff-check, and full builder tests pass.
stop_conditions: A provider call, runtime execution, route mount, DB write, file write by the app, GitHub write by the app, permit issue, merge, deploy, package change, workflow change, or retired sandbox-write reopening becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: Live operator Maya evidence shows the deployed service can prove gate readiness and render the operator dashboard, but provider, runtime, and write execution are still intentionally closed.
